response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 performs Event Grid remediation via a backend-mediated “ensure” API that calls Fleet/APIM to create or fix Event Grid subscriptions, plus a separate server-side bulk remediation system (Postgres + a timer worker) for batch work and UI continuity. The frontend drives remediation by calling the backend ensure endpoint; the backend obtains a service token and polls APIM until the ensure operation completes.

Details / flow:

- UI → backend:
  - The Event Grid Details page does not call Fleet directly in production. The frontend calls the azr-compliance-v3 backend endpoint (POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure or the bulk start endpoint) to initiate remediation.

- Backend → Fleet/APIM:
  - The backend obtains an application/service token (recently implemented by reusing the Rackspace Identity flow via shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity')) and sends Authorization: Bearer to APIM.
  - The backend calls the Fleet/APIM ensure endpoint for the target subscription (e.g. /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription) and polls the returned statusUrl until completion.

- Single-subscription remediation:
  - Backend-mediated ensure call triggers the Fleet/APIM orchestration that creates or reconciles the Event Grid subscription for the given subscription.
  - The frontend receives the final result via the backend after polling completes.

- Bulk remediation:
  - Server-side bulk remediation was added with Postgres tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items (no reuse of the old sync_queue).
  - The bulk start endpoint snapshots the current deployable subscription set from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL (so the run has a stable set to act on) and will reuse an active queued/running run if present.
  - A dedicated ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation timer worker (15s cadence) processes run items and is guarded by an advisory lock so only one worker instance acts at a time.
  - Item statuses are persisted in the DB so the UI/page refresh can show progress and the latest per-item state.

- Inventory / scoping considerations:
  - The system distinguishes deployment inventory from remediation coverage (GetEventGridDeployments returns metadata.coverage with deployable vs out-of-scope lists and counts).
  - SyncEventGrid filters to Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations (requires managed_by_tenant_id == Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and active/succeeded delegation state). The sync uses a single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and filters rows before upsert.

In short: the frontend requests remediation via the backend; the backend acquires a service token and calls/polls Fleet/APIM to perform the ensure; for bulk work azr-compliance-v3 runs a Postgres-backed snapshot + worker-based orchestration so runs are durable, visible to the UI, and processed safely.

The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explic"]}
